method.acceptanceBasis: Independent category gates; no averaging across categories
method.scoreRole: A numeric score is a derived byproduct and was not used to decide this verdict
method.passRule: A category clears only when its mapped evidence passes and no category-specific critical defect remains

judgeConsistency.summary: All 17 locked EV requirements passed in each of the three independent judge runs

categories.floor: All six surfaces expose usable keyboard and assistive-technology paths, with no critical or serious automated accessibility violation
evidence: EV-NAT-02 passed 3/3: axe-core found zero critical or serious violations on all six surfaces in Chromium and WebKit
EV-NAT-03 passed 3/3: sliders expose plain-language aria-valuetext and live announcements are debounced
EV-GATE-01 passed 3/3: keyboard-only completion, focus behavior, 44px targets and failure paths passed on WebKit
The focused academy-lesson verify report records no missing selectors, clipped text, small targets, console errors or page errors in normal and reduced-motion states

categories.floor: Live truth responds within the locked performance budget; motion semantics preserve information and reduced-motion parity
evidence: EV-MOT-01 passed 3/3: both browser engines stayed below the locked 16.7 ms p95 budget over 220 events
EV-MOT-02 and EV-MOT-03 passed 3/3: phosphor decay is confined to the trace and uses at most two monotonically fading ghosts
EV-MOT-04 passed 3/3: Aperture transition exists in normal motion and becomes an information-equivalent fade under reduced motion
Normal and reduced-motion learning state equivalence is covered by the full WebKit gate

categories.floor: Engine truth, estimates, display limits and mastery claims remain visibly and behaviorally distinct
evidence: EV-REN-02 and EV-REN-03 passed 3/3: each state has at most one engine-true annotation, motor truth uses ember and estimate layers use violet
The browser contract verifies exact clamp/floor sensitivity, sourced real-world separation, exact myth engine runs and live-engine mastery evaluation
Non-finite model inputs preserve the last valid truth and award no mission or mastery credit
impact-flight.js and www/impact-flight.js share SHA-256 7E5323C3B5C553A4D6CC12177687256D61E16F5429C9A17DF1FB49911261CB26 and have no working-tree diff

categories.floor: Each of the six paged surfaces has one clear job, an explicit next action and no essential internal-scroll dependency
evidence: EV-NAT-04 passed 3/3: the surface manifest covers Mission, Lab, Mastery and Result essential selectors
Visual inspection of 0-mission.png through 5-result.png confirms six distinct jobs: orient, manipulate, compare influence, test myths, demonstrate mastery and conclude
The focused verify report records no clipped essential text or horizontal overflow at 430x932 and 375x812

The blind pairwise comparison preferred the native generation in all four compared states; the only noted redundancy is the duplicated Launch Angle destination on Result, which does not create a competing task

categories.floor: Both portrait target sizes remain complete, touch-safe and stable across browser engines, text scaling and motion modes
evidence: EV-NAT-01 passed 3/3: all six surfaces remain intact at 130% text scale with no clipping, overlap or horizontal scroll
EV-REG-01 passed 3/3: all 48 approved visual-regression states are within the locked 0.1% threshold
EV-GATE-01 passed 3/3: all six surfaces fit 430x932 and 375x812 with native target sizes on WebKit
The focused verify report has four clean portrait viewport/motion states and zero runtime, resource, overflow, selector, target-size or clipping findings

nonBlockingObservations: The Result surface repeats the Launch Angle destination in the content card and sticky action; this is one destination and did not fail the information-architecture floor
Physical-device input-to-present timing, manual VoiceOver and the five-person five-second test remain owner-run release checkpoints; they are not autonomous category-floor failures
